content:
The shift from one human + one AI to entire teams working alongside agents is already happening. Here’s what Anthropic learned building it.

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

PC: AnthropicFor the past couple of years, working with AI meant one person, one chat window, one session at a time.
You’d open Claude or ChatGPT, ask something, get an answer, close the tab. Maybe you’d paste code in, get it fixed, move on. Simple. Effective. But still a solo experience.
That’s changing, faster than most people realize.
Anthropic just published a breakdown of how they’re actually building and using what they call “multiplayer agents” internally. Not as a product pitch. As a field report from teams that have been running these human-agent workflows for months.
I read through everything and here’s what you actually need to know.
What is a “Multiplayer Agent,” exactly?
A multiplayer agent isn’t just a chatbot that multiple people can ping. That’s just a shared inbox.
The real definition: an AI model that works alongside many different humans simultaneously, in the same workspace, with its own credentials, memory, and tools.
